reasoning: Since this is a daily average temperature comparison, we evaluate the distribution of the difference between the target dates and the base date (Oct 26). The mean daily temperature in Clermont-Ferrand follows a seasonal cycle: ~11°C in late October, ~9°C in early Nov, ~5.5°C in late Nov, and ~3.5°C in late January. The standard deviation of the difference between two essentially independent days is roughly 5.5-6.0°C. Thus, the expected expected difference (Target - Oct 26) goes from roughly -2°C (Nov 2) to -5.5°C (Nov 25) to -7.5°C (Jan 24). This strongly decreasing trend outpaces the variance, making the probability of the target date being warmer than Oct 26 naturally shrink with the further progression into winter. The estimated probabilities approximately reflect the standard normal bounds for these distributions.
